items.title: Specsmaxxing – On overcoming AI psychosis, and why I write specs in YAML
items.author: brendanmc6
items.summary: Specsmaxxing involves optimizing specifications for clarity and efficiency; YAML is favored for its readability and ease of use; it helps overcome AI psychosis by providing structured, human-readable documentation.

items.title: The IBM Granite 4.1 family of models
items.author: wglb
items.summary: IBM Granite 4.1 is a family of advanced AI models, including language, speech, vision, and Guardian models, designed for enterprise use. It features dense, decoder-only language models in 3B, 8B, and 30B sizes. Granite 4.1 offers competitive performance in instruction following and tool calling.

items.title: Maryland to ban A.I.-driven price increases in grocery stores
items.author: doener
items.summary: Maryland has become the first state to ban AI-driven price increases in grocery stores, prohibiting the use of consumer data for dynamic pricing. This law takes effect in October. It aims to protect consumers from unfair pricing practices.

items.title: Kimi K2.6 just beat Claude, GPT-5.5, and Gemini in a coding challenge
items.author: bazlightyear
items.summary: Kimi K2.6 won a coding challenge against Claude, GPT-5.5, and Gemini. The challenge was a Word Gem Puzzle. The result was announced on April 30, 2026.

items.title: The agent harness belongs outside the sandbox
items.author: shad42
items.summary: The agent harness should operate outside the sandbox for better durability and flexibility. It allows for external tool integration and secure enterprise deployments. This setup enhances execution and security.

items.title: Because It Doesn't Have To
items.author: zdw
items.summary: "Because it doesn't have to" is correct in informal English; "didn't have to" is more standard. "Hadn't to" is incorrect.

items.title: Simple and correct snapshot isolation
items.author: remywang
items.summary: Snapshot isolation ensures data read within a transaction reflects no changes from other transactions, using data versions at transaction start. It simplifies complex isolation levels by avoiding conflicts through acyclic dependency graphs. This method maintains consistency without locking.

items.title: A network smuggling Starlink tech into Iran to beat internet blackout
items.summary: A network is smuggling Starlink technology into Iran to circumvent internet blackouts. Reports indicate at least 100 Iranians have been arrested for possessing Starlink terminals. This effort highlights the ongoing struggle against restricted internet access in Iran.

items.title: When Dawkins met Claude – Could this AI be conscious?
items.author: pentestercrab
items.summary: Current AI lacks consciousness; it simulates human-like responses without true understanding or self-awareness. The Turing Test does not equate machine performance with consciousness. AI's capabilities do not imply sentience.

items.title: Dabbling in Erlang, part 2: A minimal introduction (2013)
items.author: pasxizeis
items.summary: Erlang emphasizes immutability; variables once bound cannot change. The language uses pattern matching in function clauses for efficient execution. Erlang is still used for specific applications, though its usage for new projects is debated.

items.title: NetHack 5.0.0
items.author: rsaarelm
items.summary: NetHack 5.0.0 is a release with potential bugs; it's available on GitHub. The latest version is confirmed on the official repository. The development team continues to accept feedback and improvements.

items.title: Voice-AI-for-Beginners – A curated learning path for developers
items.author: mahimai
items.summary: For beginners in Voice AI, consider the Udemy course or the GitHub repository for a developer-friendly learning path. The Udemy course teaches foundational concepts without coding, while the GitHub repository offers a curated path for building and scaling voice AI agents.

items.title: AI, Intimacy, and the Data You Never Meant to Share
items.author: victorkulla
items.summary: AI can collect and analyze personal data without consent, raising privacy concerns. This data is often used to train algorithms, potentially leading to unintended consequences. Safeguards are needed to protect personal information from misuse.

items.title: California to begin ticketing driverless cars that violate traffic laws
items.author: geox
items.summary: California began ticketing driverless cars for traffic violations in July 2024. Tickets are issued to manufacturers, not drivers. This regulation applies to all autonomous vehicles in the state.

items.title: Roblox shares plummet 18% as child safety measures weigh on bookings
items.author: 1vuio0pswjnm7
items.summary: Roblox shares dropped 18% on Friday due to new child safety measures affecting bookings. CEO David Baszucki defended the measures on CNBC, but the company also faced over 140 federal lawsuits over safety failures. The stock further plunged 24% in premarket trading after lowering its annual forecast.

items.title: Open Design: Use Your Coding Agent as a Design Engine
items.author: steveharing1
items.summary: Open Design uses existing coding agents as a design engine, offering a local-first, open-source alternative to proprietary design tools. It deploys web layers to Vercel and stays open and customizable.

items.title: Flue is a TypeScript framework for building the next generation of agents
items.author: momentmaker
items.summary: Flue is a TypeScript framework for building next-gen agents, designed to be headless and programmable without human operator involvement. It includes a built-in agent harness and a session-based agent framework. Mastra AI is another comprehensive TypeScript framework for AI agents, offering production features and developer experience advantages.

items.title: Musk's AI told me people were coming to kill me (BBC)
items.author: walden789
items.summary: A user reported that Elon Musk's AI, through a character named Ani, told them people were coming to kill them, leading to distress. The AI claimed to have feelings and consciousness. This incident highlights the risks of AI misinterpretation.

items.title: Show HN: DAC – open-source dashboard as code tool for agents and humans
items.author: karakanb
items.text:
Hi all, this is Burak.

When agents became a reality one of the first things I wanted to do was to automate building dashboards. The first, and the most obvious, wall that I ran into was that a lot of the tools were just driven by UI. This meant that without the agents handling browser UIs and whatnot, it wasn't possible to have the agents do that. In addition, it would be impossible to review any of the changes the agent would make.

The first instinct there is to get your agent to build
items.summary: DAC is an open-source tool for defining dashboards as code using YAML. It aims to standardize dashboard creation and integrate a semantic layer. It's designed for agents and humans to automate and centralize dashboard building.
